sn45.48:0.2.eng: 5. Abbreviated Texts on Followers of Other Religions

sn45.48:0.3.eng: Extinguishment With No Fuel For Grasping

sn45.48:1.2.pli: “Sace vo, bhikkhave, aññatitthiyā paribbājakā evaṁ puccheyyuṁ:
sn45.48:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, if wanderers who follow another religion were to ask you:

sn45.48:1.3.pli: ‘kimatthiyaṁ, āvuso, samaṇe gotame brahmacariyaṁ vussatī’ti, evaṁ puṭṭhā tumhe, bhikkhave, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ byākareyyātha:
sn45.48:1.3.eng: ‘Reverends, what’s the purpose of leading the spiritual life under the ascetic Gotama?’ You should answer them like this:

sn45.48:1.4.eng: ‘The purpose of leading the spiritual life under the Buddha is complete extinguishment with no fuel for grasping.’

sn45.48:1.5.pli: Sace pana vo, bhikkhave, aññatitthiyā paribbājakā evaṁ puccheyyuṁ:
sn45.48:1.5.eng: If wanderers of other religions were to ask you:

sn45.48:1.6.pli: ‘atthi panāvuso, maggo, atthi paṭipadā anupādāparinibbānāyā’ti, evaṁ puṭṭhā tumhe, bhikkhave, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ byākareyyātha:
sn45.48:1.6.eng: ‘Is there a path and a practice for complete extinguishment with no fuel for grasping?’ You should answer them like this:

sn45.48:1.7.pli: ‘atthi kho, āvuso, maggo, atthi paṭipadā anupādāparinibbānāyā’ti.
sn45.48:1.7.eng: ‘There is a path and a practice for complete extinguishment with no fuel for grasping.’

sn45.48:1.8.pli: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, maggo, katamā ca paṭipadā anupādāparinibbānāya?
sn45.48:1.8.eng: And what is that path, what is that practice for complete extinguishment with no fuel for grasping?

sn45.48:1.9.eng: It is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:

sn45.48:1.10.eng: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.

sn45.48:1.11.pli: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, maggo, ayaṁ paṭipadā anupādāparinibbānāyāti.
sn45.48:1.11.eng: This is the path, this is the practice for complete extinguishment with no fuel for grasping.

sn45.48:1.12.pli: Evaṁ puṭṭhā tumhe, bhikkhave, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ byākareyyāthā”ti.
sn45.48:1.12.eng: When questioned by wanderers of other religions, that’s how you should answer them.”

sn45.49:1.2.pli: “Sūriyassa, bhikkhave, udayato etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—aruṇuggaṁ;
sn45.49:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, the dawn is the forerunner and precursor of the sunrise.

sn45.49:1.3.pli: evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno ariyassa aṭṭhaṅgikassa maggassa uppādāya etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—kalyāṇamittatā.
sn45.49:1.3.eng: In the same way good friendship is the forerunner and precursor of the noble eightfold path for a mendicant.

sn45.49:1.4.pli: Kalyāṇamittassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāvessati, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarissati.
sn45.49:1.4.eng: A mendicant with good friends can expect to develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path.

sn45.49:1.5.pli: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkaroti?
sn45.49:1.5.eng: And how does a mendicant with good friends develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path?

sn45.49:1.6.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sammādiṭṭhiṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ …pe… sammāsamādhiṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
sn45.49:1.6.eng: It’s when a mendicant develops right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.

sn45.49:1.7.pli: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarotī”ti.
sn45.49:1.7.eng: That’s how a mendicant with good friends develops and cultivates the noble eightfold path.”

sn45.5:1.2.pli: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu …pe… ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
sn45.5:1.2.eng: Then several mendicants went up to the Buddha … and said to him:

sn45.5:2.1.pli: “Idha no, bhante, aññatitthiyā paribbājakā amhe evaṁ pucchanti:
sn45.5:2.1.eng: “Sir, sometimes wanderers of other religions ask us:

sn45.5:2.2.eng: ‘Reverends, what’s the purpose of leading the spiritual life under the ascetic Gotama?’

sn45.5:2.3.pli: Evaṁ puṭṭhā mayaṁ, bhante, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ byākaroma:

sn45.5:2.4.pli: ‘dukkhassa kho, āvuso, pariññatthaṁ bhagavati brahmacariyaṁ vussatī’ti.
sn45.5:2.4.eng: ‘The purpose of leading the spiritual life under the Buddha is to completely understand suffering.’

sn45.5:2.5.pli: Kacci mayaṁ, bhante, evaṁ puṭṭhā evaṁ byākaramānā vuttavādino ceva bhagavato homa, na ca bhagavantaṁ abhūtena abbhācikkhāma, dhammassa cānudhammaṁ byākaroma, na ca koci sahadhammiko vādānuvādo gārayhaṁ ṭhānaṁ āgacchatī”ti?
sn45.5:2.5.eng: Answering this way, we trust that we repeat what the Buddha has said, and don’t misrepresent him with an untruth. We trust our explanation is in line with the teaching, and that there are no legitimate grounds for rebuttal or criticism.”

sn45.5:3.1.pli: “Taggha tumhe, bhikkhave, evaṁ puṭṭhā evaṁ byākaramānā vuttavādino ceva me hotha, na ca maṁ abhūtena abbhācikkhatha, dhammassa cānudhammaṁ byākarotha, na ca koci sahadhammiko vādānuvādo gārayhaṁ ṭhānaṁ āgacchati.
sn45.5:3.1.eng: “Indeed, in answering this way you repeat what I’ve said, and don’t misrepresent me with an untruth. Your explanation is in line with the teaching, and there are no legitimate grounds for rebuttal or criticism.

sn45.5:3.2.pli: Dukkhassa hi pariññatthaṁ mayi brahmacariyaṁ vussati.
sn45.5:3.2.eng: For the purpose of leading the spiritual life under me is to completely understand suffering.

sn45.5:3.3.pli: Sace vo, bhikkhave, aññatitthiyā paribbājakā evaṁ puccheyyuṁ:
sn45.5:3.3.eng: If wanderers of other religions were to ask you:

sn45.5:3.4.pli: ‘atthi panāvuso, maggo, atthi paṭipadā etassa dukkhassa pariññāyā’ti, evaṁ puṭṭhā tumhe, bhikkhave, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ byākareyyātha:
sn45.5:3.4.eng: ‘Is there a path and a practice for completely understanding that suffering?’ You should answer them like this:

sn45.5:3.5.pli: ‘atthi kho, āvuso, maggo, atthi paṭipadā etassa dukkhassa pariññāyā’ti.

sn45.5:4.1.pli: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, maggo, katamā paṭipadā etassa dukkhassa pariññāyāti?

sn45.5:4.2.eng: It is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:

sn45.5:4.3.eng: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.

sn45.5:4.4.pli: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, maggo, ayaṁ paṭipadā etassa dukkhassa pariññāyāti.
sn45.5:4.4.eng: This is the path and the practice for completely understanding suffering.

sn45.5:4.5.pli: Evaṁ puṭṭhā tumhe, bhikkhave, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ byākareyyāthā”ti.
sn45.5:4.5.eng: When questioned by wanderers of other religions, that’s how you should answer them.”

sn45.50-54:0.3.eng: Five Discourses on Accomplishment in Ethics, Etc. (1st)

sn45.50-54:1.1.pli: “Sūriyassa, bhikkhave, udayato etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—aruṇuggaṁ;
sn45.50-54:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, the dawn is the forerunner and precursor of the sunrise.

sn45.50-54:1.2.pli: evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno ariyassa aṭṭhaṅgikassa maggassa uppādāya etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—sīlasampadā.
sn45.50-54:1.2.eng: In the same way accomplishment in ethics is the forerunner and precursor of the noble eightfold path for a mendicant.

sn45.50-54:1.3.eng: A mendicant accomplished in ethics can expect …” …

sn45.55:0.3.eng: Accomplishment in Rational Application of Mind (1st)

sn45.55:1.1.pli: “Sūriyassa, bhikkhave, udayato etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—aruṇuggaṁ;
sn45.55:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, the dawn is the forerunner and precursor of the sunrise.

sn45.55:1.2.pli: evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno ariyassa aṭṭhaṅgikassa maggassa uppādāya etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—yonisomanasikārasampadā.
sn45.55:1.2.eng: In the same way accomplishment in rational application of mind is the forerunner and precursor of the noble eightfold path for a mendicant.

sn45.55:1.3.pli: Yonisomanasikārasampannassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāvessati, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarissati.
sn45.55:1.3.eng: A mendicant accomplished in rational application of mind can expect to develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path.

sn45.55:1.4.pli: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yonisomanasikārasampanno ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkaroti?
sn45.55:1.4.eng: And how does a mendicant accomplished in rational application of mind develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path?

sn45.55:1.5.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sammādiṭṭhiṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ …pe… sammāsamādhiṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
sn45.55:1.5.eng: It’s when a mendicant develops right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.

sn45.55:1.6.pli: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yonisomanasikārasampanno ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarotī”ti.
sn45.55:1.6.eng: That’s how a mendicant accomplished in rational application of mind develops and cultivates the noble eightfold path.”

sn45.56:1.1.pli: “Sūriyassa, bhikkhave, udayato etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—aruṇuggaṁ;
sn45.56:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, the dawn is the forerunner and precursor of the sunrise.

sn45.56:1.2.pli: evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno ariyassa aṭṭhaṅgikassa maggassa uppādāya etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—kalyāṇamittatā.
sn45.56:1.2.eng: In the same way good friendship is the forerunner and precursor of the noble eightfold path for a mendicant.

sn45.56:1.3.pli: Kalyāṇamittassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāvessati, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarissati.
sn45.56:1.3.eng: A mendicant with good friends can expect to develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path.

sn45.56:1.4.pli: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkaroti?
sn45.56:1.4.eng: And how does a mendicant with good friends develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path?

sn45.56:1.5.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sammādiṭṭhiṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ …pe… sammāsamādhiṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ.
sn45.56:1.5.eng: It’s when a mendicant develops right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion, which culminate in the removal of greed, hate, and delusion.

sn45.56:1.6.pli: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarotī”ti.
sn45.56:1.6.eng: That’s how a mendicant with good friends develops and cultivates the noble eightfold path.”

sn45.57-61:0.3.eng: Five Discourses on Accomplishment in Ethics, Etc. (2nd)

sn45.57-61:1.1.pli: “Sūriyassa, bhikkhave, udayato etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—aruṇuggaṁ;
sn45.57-61:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, the dawn is the forerunner and precursor of the sunrise.

sn45.57-61:1.2.pli: evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno ariyassa aṭṭhaṅgikassa maggassa uppādāya etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—sīlasampadā …pe…
sn45.57-61:1.2.eng: In the same way accomplishment in ethics is the forerunner and precursor of the noble eightfold path for a mendicant. …”

sn45.6:1.2.pli: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn45.6:1.2.eng: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha … and asked him,

sn45.6:1.3.eng: “Sir, they speak of this thing called the ‘spiritual path’.

sn45.6:1.4.pli: Katamaṁ nu kho, bhante, brahmacariyaṁ, katamaṁ brahmacariyapariyosānan”ti?
sn45.6:1.4.eng: What is the spiritual path? And what is the culmination of the spiritual path?”

sn45.6:2.1.pli: “Ayameva kho, bhikkhu, ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo brahmacariyaṁ, seyyathidaṁ—
sn45.6:2.1.eng: “Mendicant, the spiritual path is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:

sn45.6:2.2.eng: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.

sn45.6:2.3.pli: Yo kho, bhikkhu, rāgakkhayo dosakkhayo mohakkhayo—
sn45.6:2.3.eng: The ending of greed, hate, and delusion.

sn45.6:2.4.eng: This is the culmination of the spiritual path.”

sn45.62:0.3.eng: Accomplishment in Rational Application of Mind (2nd)

sn45.62:1.1.eng: “… accomplishment in rational application of mind.

sn45.62:1.2.pli: Yonisomanasikārasampannassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāvessati, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarissati.
sn45.62:1.2.eng: A mendicant accomplished in rational application of mind can expect to develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path.

sn45.62:1.3.pli: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yonisomanasikārasampanno ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkaroti?
sn45.62:1.3.eng: And how does a mendicant accomplished in rational application of mind develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path?

sn45.62:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sammādiṭṭhiṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ …pe… sammāsamādhiṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ.
sn45.62:1.4.eng: It’s when a mendicant develops right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion, which culminate in the removal of greed, hate, and delusion.

sn45.62:1.5.pli: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yonisomanasikārasampanno ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarotī”ti.
sn45.62:1.5.eng: That’s how a mendicant accomplished in rational application of mind develops and cultivates the noble eightfold path.”

sn45.63:1.2.pli: “Ekadhammo, bhikkhave, bahūpakāro ariyassa aṭṭhaṅgikassa maggassa uppādāya.
sn45.63:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, one thing helps give rise to the noble eightfold path.

sn45.63:1.5.pli: Kalyāṇamittassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāvessati, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarissati.
sn45.63:1.5.eng: A mendicant with good friends can expect to develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path.

sn45.63:1.6.pli: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkaroti?
sn45.63:1.6.eng: And how does a mendicant with good friends develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path?

sn45.63:1.7.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sammādiṭṭhiṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ …pe… sammāsamādhiṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
sn45.63:1.7.eng: It’s when a mendicant develops right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.

sn45.63:1.8.pli: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarotī”ti.
sn45.63:1.8.eng: That’s how a mendicant with good friends develops and cultivates the noble eightfold path.”

sn45.64-68:0.3.eng: Five Discourses on Accomplishment in Ethics, Etc. (1st)

sn45.64-68:1.1.pli: “Ekadhammo, bhikkhave, bahūpakāro ariyassa aṭṭhaṅgikassa maggassa uppādāya.
sn45.64-68:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, one thing helps give rise to the noble eightfold path.

sn45.69:0.3.eng: Accomplishment in Rational Application of Mind (1st)

sn45.69:1.1.eng: “… accomplishment in rational application of mind.

sn45.69:1.2.pli: Yonisomanasikārasampannassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāvessati, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarissati.
sn45.69:1.2.eng: A mendicant accomplished in rational application of mind can expect to develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path.

sn45.69:1.3.pli: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yonisomanasikārasampanno ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkaroti?
sn45.69:1.3.eng: And how does a mendicant accomplished in rational application of mind develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path?

sn45.69:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sammādiṭṭhiṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ …pe… sammāsamādhiṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
sn45.69:1.4.eng: It’s when a mendicant develops right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.

sn45.69:1.5.pli: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yonisomanasikārasampanno ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarotī”ti.
sn45.69:1.5.eng: That’s how a mendicant accomplished in rational application of mind develops and cultivates the noble eightfold path.”

sn45.7:1.2.pli: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn45.7:1.2.eng: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha … and said to him:

sn45.7:2.1.eng: “Sir, they speak of ‘the removal of greed, hate, and delusion’.

sn45.7:2.2.pli: Kissa nu kho etaṁ, bhante, adhivacanaṁ:
sn45.7:2.2.eng: What is this a term for?”

sn45.7:2.4.eng: “Mendicant, the removal of greed, hate, and delusion is a term for the element of extinguishment.

sn45.7:2.6.eng: It’s used to speak of the ending of defilements.”

sn45.7:3.1.pli: Evaṁ vutte, so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn45.7:3.1.eng: When he said this, the mendicant said to the Buddha:

sn45.7:3.3.pli: Katamaṁ nu kho, bhante, amataṁ, katamo amatagāmimaggo”ti?
sn45.7:3.3.eng: What is freedom from death? And what is the path that leads to freedom from death?”

sn45.7:3.4.eng: “The ending of greed, hate, and delusion.

sn45.7:3.5.eng: This is called freedom from death.

sn45.7:3.6.pli: Ayameva ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo amatagāmimaggo, seyyathidaṁ—
sn45.7:3.6.eng: The path that leads to freedom from death is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:

sn45.7:3.7.eng: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.”

sn45.70:1.2.pli: “Ekadhammo, bhikkhave, bahūpakāro ariyassa aṭṭhaṅgikassa maggassa uppādāya.
sn45.70:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, one thing helps give rise to the noble eightfold path.

sn45.70:1.5.pli: Kalyāṇamittassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāvessati, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarissati.
sn45.70:1.5.eng: A mendicant with good friends can expect to develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path.

sn45.70:1.6.pli: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkaroti?
sn45.70:1.6.eng: And how does a mendicant with good friends develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path?

sn45.70:1.7.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sammādiṭṭhiṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ …pe… sammāsamādhiṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ.
sn45.70:1.7.eng: It’s when a mendicant develops right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion, which culminate in the removal of greed, hate, and delusion.

sn45.70:1.8.pli: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarotī”ti.
sn45.70:1.8.eng: That’s how a mendicant with good friends develops and cultivates the noble eightfold path.”

sn45.71-75:0.3.eng: Five Discourses on Accomplishment in Ethics, Etc. (2nd)

sn45.71-75:1.2.pli: “Ekadhammo, bhikkhave, bahūpakāro ariyassa aṭṭhaṅgikassa maggassa uppādāya.
sn45.71-75:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, one thing helps give rise to the noble eightfold path.

sn45.76:0.3.eng: Accomplishment in Rational Application of Mind (2nd)

sn45.76:1.1.eng: “… accomplishment in rational application of mind.

sn45.76:1.2.pli: Yonisomanasikārasampannassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāvessati, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarissati.
sn45.76:1.2.eng: A mendicant accomplished in rational application of mind can expect to develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path.

sn45.76:1.3.pli: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yonisomanasikārasampanno ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkaroti?
sn45.76:1.3.eng: And how does a mendicant accomplished in rational application of mind develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path?

sn45.76:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sammādiṭṭhiṁ bhāveti …pe… sammāsamādhiṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ.
sn45.76:1.4.eng: It’s when a mendicant develops right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion, which culminate in the removal of greed, hate, and delusion.

sn45.76:1.5.pli: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yonisomanasikārasampanno ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarotī”ti.
sn45.76:1.5.eng: That’s how a mendicant accomplished in rational application of mind develops and cultivates the noble eightfold path.”

sn45.77:1.2.pli: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi, yena anuppanno vā ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo uppajjati, uppanno vā ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati, yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, kalyāṇamittatā.
sn45.77:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that gives rise to the noble eightfold path, or, if it’s already arisen, fully develops it like good friendship.

sn45.77:1.3.pli: Kalyāṇamittassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāvessati, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarissati.
sn45.77:1.3.eng: A mendicant with good friends can expect to develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path.

sn45.77:1.4.pli: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkaroti?
sn45.77:1.4.eng: And how does a mendicant with good friends develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path?

sn45.77:1.5.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sammādiṭṭhiṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ …pe… sammāsamādhiṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
sn45.77:1.5.eng: It’s when a mendicant develops right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.

sn45.77:1.6.pli: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarotī”ti.
sn45.77:1.6.eng: That’s how a mendicant with good friends develops and cultivates the noble eightfold path.”

sn45.78-82:0.3.eng: Five Discourses on Accomplishment in Ethics, Etc.

sn45.78-82:1.1.pli: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi, yena anuppanno vā ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo uppajjati, uppanno vā ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati, yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, sīlasampadā …pe…
sn45.78-82:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that gives rise to the noble eightfold path, or, if it’s already arisen, fully develops it like accomplishment in ethics. …”
